allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking helps butterfly-effect map how small changes travel across interdependent parts instead of treating events in isolation.
allies.source_quote: Systems Thinking ... requires seeing interrelationships rather than isolated items.

allies.rationale_text: Second-order-thinking makes butterfly-effect actionable by forcing attention onto downstream consequences instead of only immediate outcomes.
allies.source_quote: The model compels us to engage in second-order thinking and visualize the entire chain of consequences.

antagonists.rationale_text: Occams-razor can suppress butterfly-effect when preference for simple explanations hides the intertwined causal chains that matter in nonlinear systems.
antagonists.source_quote: Occam’s Razor ... clashes directly with the premise of the Butterfly Effect.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Butterfly-effect conflicts with status-quo-bias when small early signals are ignored until the systemic impact has already compounded.
structured_tensions.source_quote: butterfly-effect vs status-quo-bias: butterfly-effect conflicts with status-quo-bias (status quo bias) when small early signals are ignored until systemic impact compounds.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Butterfly-effect conflicts with representativeness-heuristic when local similarity hides nonlinear cascade risk.
structured_tensions.source_quote: butterfly-effect vs representativeness-heuristic: butterfly-effect conflicts with representativeness-heuristic (representativeness heuristic) when local similarity hides nonlinear cascade risk.

curation_notes.summary: Preserved the clearest systems and second-order allies plus the explicit anti-status-quo and anti-resemblance tension block.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader root-cause and scenario-sprawl beyond the clearest current-vocabulary seams.
open_questions: Confirmation-bias: Task 4.2 re-evaluated — no explicit mention found in source markdown. Keep omitted.
